Complete and Continue

    Keith M Hilgenfeldt
    2 years ago
    Reply
    Link
    I feel like this for the fist time in 20 years I feel ln a flow I can not put real words too. I began this Mystery school 2 months ago and I choose God mode week one. I have committed to minimum 6 hours a day on my photography and I have now completed 60 days consecutively. I just took my first day off this past Saturday and it felt weird so i still worked a couple of hours. I never knew this level of opportunity existed in the world and its all around you no matter where you are. Take the time look around and see what is on your path in real time. Stop thinking about being successful in the future and screw the past that is gone. Stay in the now and you will see it unfold right in front of you and all around you. Commit to your Art, its worth it!!
    Ashris Choudhury

      fucking real. I didn’t think I would live through childhood but here I am at a young adult alive. I’m not in fight or flight mode anymore. Dreams and goals are for me. I’m here so now I have to do it for little kid me who didn’t know there would be a tomorrow, for right now me who deserves better, and for future me who wants to uplift others

                          I've always thought that the reason I procrastinate sometimes is because I am a perfectionist and I don't want to make something that maybe won't be a perfect, beautiful work of art. Maybe it is just because I don't know what or how the first or next step will go. Maybe it's because I don't know how to start or don't know what I'm doing!. Maybe it's all of the above.

                            None of the above. It's because you don't "know" that "knowing" isn't important. Action is.                                                 Cody James
                                                5 years ago
                                                Reply
                                                Link
                                                Today marks the first week as a full time creative. Last week I created my daily and weekly schedule with goals and objectives built in. That alone was way out of my comfort zone. Making a plan? Establishing goals? Forget it. The old me was a flopping fish gasping for breath. Starting over in the Mystery School is part of that curriculum. Digging in, following through, and finishing is how I'm using this to transform myself. The old me would just keep going, aimlessly searching. The old me is dead.
                                                Ashris Choudhury
